rationale: This is a classic and simple example of the Hofmann-Löffler-Freytag reaction. The substrate, N-chloro-N-methylbutan-1-amine, undergoes a 1,5-hydrogen atom transfer upon treatment with acid (e.g., sulfuric acid) and light/heat, followed by cyclization to form the product, N-methylpyrrolidine. This illustrates the key remote C-H functionalization and pyrrolidine ring formation characteristic of the reaction.
collision_avoidance_note: The reaction involves an N-haloamine substrate and an intramolecular C-H activation/cyclization to form a pyrrolidine. This specific transformation is highly characteristic of the Hofmann-Löffler-Freytag reaction and avoids common motifs like arenes, diazo groups, or decarboxylations that could cause collisions with other named reactions. The acyclic substrate and simple pyrrolidine product keep the example focused and canonical.
